briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — finesse summer approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for summer with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use big baits on a Carolina Rig for bigger fish offshore — 10-inch worms, magnum flukes, and full-sized creature baits. Fish it on the same hard spots as a football jig. Use an 8.1:1 reel to pick up slack quickly and a heavy flipping rod.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use big baits on a Carolina Rig for bigger fish offshore — 10-inch worms, magnum flukes, and full-sized creature baits. Fish it on the same hard spots as a football jig. Use an 8.1:1 reel to
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on ledge, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on ledge, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Nobody remembers who finishes second in the Classic, so throw big baits and go for the win. Expect big swimbaits, deep-diving crankbaits, Carolina rigs, and football jigs to be the best chances.
Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

KVD: In summer on grass lakes, big fish move offshore to the middle of the lake where the bait is. They don't stay near shore when it's hot. Look for scattered hydrilla clumps in the middle of the lake.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal summer fish retreat to reliable offshore structure at 12-25ft. They're less aggressive but still need to eat. Focus on primary structure — main lake points, ledges, and humps with shade or current.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
